name: Writing Styles
description: A single Writing Style wildcard with 15 distinct flavors — roll one to retell any situation in a fresh voice.

items.name: Writing Style
items.description: Stylistic voice/flavor to render a scene or situation in. Use as 'Writing style: %%Writing Style%%' to retell the same situation different ways.

entries.text: Hardboiled noir — rain-slicked streets, cigarette smoke, and clipped, cynical narration where everyone has an angle
Whimsical fairy tale — once-upon-a-time cadence, threes and repetitions, and a gentle moral humming beneath the surface
Spare Hemingway minimalism — short declarative sentences, concrete nouns, no adverbs, the real feeling left unsaid under the iceberg
Victorian Gothic — long candlelit sentences, dread and decay, crumbling estates and the weight of secrets in every shadow
Breathless tabloid sensationalism — ALL-CAPS shockers, exclamation points, lurid speculation, and a cliffhanger every other line
Dry technical manual — numbered steps, passive voice, precise tolerances, and an utter refusal to acknowledge any human emotion
Epic high-fantasy saga — archaic diction, sweeping prophecy, named swords and ancient bloodlines, the fate of kingdoms in the balance
Beat-poetry stream of consciousness — breathless run-ons, jazz rhythm, lowercase urgency, & sudden holy ampersand epiphanies
Cozy children's bedtime story — warm simple words, soft repetition, friendly animals, and everything turning out gently okay
Cyberpunk pulp — neon-drenched megacity grime, chrome and wetware, corporate dread, and slang that drips with attitude
Shakespearean blank verse — iambic pentameter, thee and thou, soaring metaphor, and asides delivered straight to the audience
Corporate press release — upbeat synergy-speak, bullet-point wins, carefully hedged optimism, and a quote from a smiling executive
Solemn true-crime documentary narration — measured present-tense voiceover, ominous foreshadowing, and 'but what investigators didn't yet know...'
Magical realism — the impossible reported with a shrug, marigolds and memory, time folding softly, the miraculous treated as ordinary weather
Deadpan absurdist comedy — flat understated delivery, escalating nonsense treated as routine, and a punchline that arrives sideways
